mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so when policy stays at or near the zero bound, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: If 20d S&P 500 forward returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS is stable or falling, or if triggered windows show negative median returns in more than half of rolling 2-year samples, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent trend marks labor-market deterioration and lower expected household income, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are positive in more than 60% of UNRATE-rise trigger events, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher RSAFS growth reflects stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should support equity earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive RSAFS acceleration do not produce above-baseline 60d S&P 500 returns, or if the rank correlation is below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when production growth is broad-based.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after a negative 3-month stretch
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO reversal events fail to outperform unconditional 20d returns, or if the median forward return after the trigger is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in HOUST and RSAFS signals simultaneous housing and consumption demand recovery, which should lift cyclically sensitive earnings and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined positive HOUST-and-RSAFS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 60d returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-impulse windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE confirms labor-market tightening and stronger household income growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition fails to beat baseline 20d returns, or if either proxy alone explains the sign better than the combined rule.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains unusually low, real policy rates fall and liquidity conditions loosen, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the low-real-rate condition does not produce positive 5d returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in GDPC1 growth after a prior expansion signals weakening aggregate demand and lower earnings revisions, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1% after being above 2% in the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if growth-slowdown quarters do not precede weaker 60d returns than the unconditional sample, or if the median post-trigger return is non-negative.
